name: Bake-Sale Lemon Meringue Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 refrigerated pie crust (half of 15-ounce, room temperature package)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Position the rack in the center.
2. Unfold the pie crust and press out the fold lines. If the crust cracks, wet your fingers and push the edges together.
3. Sprinkle flour over the crust. Place the floured side down in a 9-inch-diameter glass pie dish. Fold the edges over and crimp decoratively. Pierce the crust all over with a fork.
4. Bake until the crust is pale golden, about 12 minutes. Let it cool completely on a rack.
5. In a heavy medium saucepan, whisk together 1 1/4 cups sugar, water, 5 tablespoons cornstarch, egg yolks, lemon peel, and salt. Whisk over medium heat until the mixture comes to a boil. Continue whisking until the mixture thickens, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat.
6. Add lemon juice and butter; whisk until smooth. Let it cool completely, stirring occasionally, for about 1 hour.
7. Preheat the oven to 350°F. In a small bowl, mix 1/3 cup sugar and 1 tablespoon cornstarch.
8. Beat egg whites in a large bowl with cream of tartar until foamy. Add the sugar mixture one tablespoonful at a time, beating until soft peaks form after each addition.
9. Spread the cooled lemon filling into the crust. Spoon dollops of meringue around the edge of the pie on top of the filling. Place remaining meringue in the center and spread it to cover the filling, mounding slightly in the center and sealing completely to the crust edge.
10. Using a rubber spatula or spoon, swirl the meringue decoratively, forming peaks. Bake the pie until the meringue peaks are light brown, about 12 minutes. Let cool on a rack before refrigerating the pie until cold for about 1 1/2 hours. (Can be prepared up to 3 hours ahead and kept refrigerated.)
